text: Empire State Building climbers: Angela Nikolau and Ivan Kuznetsov, AKA Beerkus, get supervised release until next court date - ABC7 New York
fullText: The climbers, identified as Angela Nikolau and Ivan Kuznetsov, appeared in court in Lower Manhattan on Thursday.

text: Former Olympian faces felony charge over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism - The Washington Post
fullText: A grand jury indicted David Hearn, who has said he was wrongfully arrested last month. He said he reached into the water after noticing a piece of the pool’s blue liner had become partially detached.

text: Carney backs West Coast pipeline in win for Alberta and oil industry - Politico
fullText: The project is intended to ease separatist tensions in Alberta as it expands Canada’s capacity to export oil.

text: Outside Taylor Swift’s Rehearsal Dinner, a Surprising Calm — and Security Worthy of Gaddafi - The Hollywood Reporter

text: Bessent on Trump's crypto earnings: "I don't think there's an appearance problem" - CBS News
fullText: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent touched on the recent disclosure of President Trump's crypto earnings, the latest developments with the tax-deferred Trump Accounts, and the struggles facing the U.S. economy.

text: Ronaldo scores as Portugal come back to win, Croatia denied by late VAR - Al Jazeera
fullText: Cristiano Ronaldo pulls Portugal level before Goncalo Ramos wins it late on; Croatia have further goal ruled offside.

text: Sony’s PlayStation disc factory is already being repurposed - The Verge
fullText: After killing off PlayStation discs, Sony’s last disc plant in Thalgau, Austria will retrain all 300 employees to make microlenses instead.

text: Britney Spears Keeps Low Profile During Holiday Weekend Outing - TMZ

text: 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life - Fox News
fullText: University of Minnesota researchers say "SpudCell" is the most life-like synthetic cell yet, able to grow, divide and pass traits to offspring.

text: Cavalli, Contreras, 2 others disciplined for Nats-Red Sox dustup - ESPN
fullText: Cade Cavalli and Willson Contreras each have been suspended seven games for their roles in a benches-clearing brawl at Fenway Park on Tuesday night. Miles Mikolas received a five-game suspension, and Red Sox outfielder Nate Eaton got a three-game ban.

text: Ebola treatments trial begins in the Democratic Republic of Congo - BBC
fullText: According to WHO data, there have been 1,406 confirmed cases of the disease in DRC, with 301 suspected cases and 438 deaths.

text: Critics rave over Madonna’s ‘Confessions II’ - KTLA
